title: Fix infinite 'Loading your profile…' spinner on app load

requirements.text: Fix the app startup hang where ChatView displays an infinite 'Loading your profile...' spinner and never transitions to the main UI. The profile loading state must resolve within a reasonable timeout (e.g., 10 seconds) regardless of whether the backend returns a profile, returns null, or errors. If the backend call fails or returns no profile, ChatView must exit the loading state and either show the DisplayNamePrompt/ProfileSetupModal for new users or render the main UI for returning users — the spinner must never block the UI indefinitely.

quotes: It's been like this for 10 minutes

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Loading your profile...' spinner is never displayed for more than 10 seconds under any network or backend condition.
If the backend getUserProfile call resolves with a null/empty result, ChatView exits the loading state and shows the profile setup flow (DisplayNamePrompt or ProfileSetupModal).
If the backend getUserProfile call resolves with a valid profile, ChatView exits the loading state and renders the full main UI (message feed, friends sidebar, navigation).
If the backend getUserProfile call errors or times out, ChatView exits the loading state gracefully and shows an appropriate fallback (setup flow or an error message), not a permanent spinner.
On subsequent visits where a profile already exists, the app loads past the spinner quickly and renders the main chat UI without requiring any user action.
No unhandled promise rejections or console errors occur during the profile loading lifecycle.

requirements.text: Audit the useQueries.ts getUserProfile hook and the ChatView loading gate logic to identify why the loading state never resolves. Common causes to check and fix: (1) the React Query query never transitions out of 'loading' status because the actor is not yet initialised when the query fires; (2) the query is dependent on an actor ref that stays null, causing it to remain in a pending/loading state forever; (3) ChatView checks a loading flag that is never set to false when the query result is null or undefined. Ensure the query has a proper enabled condition, a sensible staleTime/retry policy, and that ChatView's loading gate correctly handles all terminal query states (success with data, success with null, and error).
acceptanceCriteria: The getUserProfile React Query hook has an 'enabled' condition that prevents it from firing before the actor is ready, and correctly transitions to a non-loading state once the actor resolves.
ChatView's loading gate (the condition that shows the spinner) evaluates to false as soon as the profile query reaches any terminal state (success or error), not just when data is truthy.
If the actor initialisation itself hangs, a timeout or fallback mechanism ensures the UI is never permanently blocked.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend Motoko actor interface or data schema.
nonGoals: Adding new chat or friends features.
Changing the visual theme or layout once the loading issue is resolved.
